title: For a Child Lost
scripture_text: See that you do not despise one of these little ones; for I tell you, their angels in heaven always behold the face of my Father.
reflection: No parent should bury a child. The order of things is wrong, and no theology fully makes it right. But God is with you in the wrongness.
body:
I do not have good words. I do not have a verse to make this okay. There is no okay.

There is only this: the Lord of hosts is near to the brokenhearted. He does not fix it. He does not explain it. He weeps with you, because His heart breaks at the same things yours does.

You will wake up tomorrow. That, in itself, will be a kind of grace. You will breathe. You will put one foot in front of the other. Not because you are brave, but because He carries you when you cannot carry yourself.

Your child is not lost. They are held. By hands bigger than yours.
prayer: Lord, hold my child. And hold me. We are both lost without each other. Be our meeting place. Amen.
